### Post by ardchoille42 on 2007-04-15
This may be a dumb question, but after you did "sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg" did you restart x? You have to restart x for the changes to take effect. You can restart x with:

```

sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart

```

Be aware that x will close and restart, so any app you have open will quit when you do that.

---

### Post by mthakur2006 on 2007-04-15
thanks for the quick reply. yes i did restart x using ctrl+alt+bkspace....
any suggestions?

---

### Post by Pikestaff on 2007-04-15
When you reconfigured xorg... when you got to the part where it asked what resolutions you wanted, did you press spacebar to pick the right ones?  I know it's kind of a silly question but when I was starting out with Ubuntu, it took me forever to figure out that you had to use the spacebar.  After that, reboot and see if it fixes anything.

---

### Post by craz on 2007-04-15
I believe it will help if you find the specs to your monitor online, then set the horizontal sync and vertical refresh rate in xorg to the values supported by your specific monitor.

---

### Post by craz on 2007-04-16
According to that a higher resolution that 1024 x 768 is not available to the LCD, only for an external monitor.  From your initial post, it seems as if you want a lower resolution?  Please clarify.  Thanks.

It may also have to do with the fact that the driver for your integrated graphics chipset doesn't support other resolutions.

---

### Post by mthakur2006 on 2007-04-16
i want a higher resolution please.
thanks.

---

### Post by craz on 2007-04-17
OK.  Try this.  Make a backup of xorg.conf and then change the HorizSync and VertiRefresh to this:
```
HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0 VertRefresh     50.0 - 160.0
``` Then log out and log back in and see if you can change your resolution.  If not, just put the settings back the what they were before and we can go through your problem some more.

---

### Post by mthakur2006 on 2007-04-17
hi craz,
i tried that and logged out and back in again. resolution did not change. then i thought of trying to changing the resolution to a lower one (just to see if it can change at all) it did not but the refresh rate did go up to 85Hz from 60 Hz (even though the resolution stayed the same). I changed it back to what it was (according to your instructions).
what do i do now?
thanks in advance.

---

### Post by craz on 2007-04-17
If the xorg change did not mess up your system, it is fine to keep the edits if you want, as you can benefit from higher refresh rates.  Don't quote me on this, as it is mearly my belief, but I believe that the only way you can get a higher resolution would be to try finding a different driver for your integrated graphics chipset.

---

i will have a look and keep it posted.
